text:
Docker is a platform for packaging applications into containers. A container includes the application code, runtime, dependencies, and configuration needed to run consistently across machines.

A Dockerfile defines how to build an image. The image can then be run as a container. Common Docker commands include docker build, docker run, docker ps, and docker logs.

Docker helps AI projects because model-serving apps often need specific Python versions, system packages, and environment variables. Containers make deployment more reproducible.

FastAPI is a modern Python web framework for building APIs. It is designed around type hints, automatic validation, and automatic API documentation. FastAPI is often used to expose machine learning models or backend services through HTTP endpoints.

A FastAPI app usually defines route functions with decorators such as @app.get or @app.post. Request and response data can be modeled with Pydantic classes. FastAPI can run with Uvicorn during development.

FastAPI is useful for AI systems because it can wrap model calls, retrieval pipelines, tools, and database access behind clean API endpoints.

Kubernetes is a container orchestration platform. It manages containerized applications across multiple machines. Kubernetes can restart failed containers, scale workloads, expose services, and manage deployments.

Important Kubernetes objects include Pods, Deployments, Services, ConfigMaps, Secrets, and Ingress resources. A Deployment manages replicas of an application. A Service provides stable networking for Pods.

Kubernetes is useful for production AI systems when applications need scaling, reliability, and controlled deployments.

PostgreSQL is a relational database known for reliability, SQL support, indexing, transactions, and extensibility. It stores structured data in tables with rows and columns.

Applications use SQL queries to insert, update, delete, and retrieve records. PostgreSQL supports indexes, joins, constraints, JSON fields, and full-text search.

AI applications often use PostgreSQL for user data, conversation metadata, application records, analytics, and sometimes vector search through extensions such as pgvector.

Python is a general-purpose programming language known for readable syntax and a large ecosystem. It is commonly used for automation, data analysis, web applications, scripting, and artificial intelligence projects.

Python programs are usually organized into modules and packages. A module is a single Python file, while a package is a folder containing related modules. Python uses indentation to define code blocks, which makes the structure visually clear.

Python is popular in AI because libraries such as NumPy, pandas, PyTorch, TensorFlow, scikit-learn, FastAPI, and Streamlit make it easy to build useful systems quickly.
